diagrams.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm Workflow
diagrams.description: Shows the step-by-step flow of Dijkstra's algorithm from initialization to termination
elements: Extract minimum distance vertex from priority queue
Relax all edges from extracted vertex
Update priority queue with new distances
Repeat until queue is empty or all reachable vertices processed
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the algorithmic flow and when to stop

diagrams.title: Relaxation Operation Visualized
diagrams.description: Shows how edge relaxation updates distances when a shorter path is found
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the core operation that computes shortest distances

diagrams.title: Why Negative Weights Fail
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how negative edge weights cause Dijkstra to produce incorrect results
elements: But Dijkstra locks B at distance 2 before processing C
Final dist[C] should be 0 but Dijkstra gives ∞ or wrong value
Shows the greedy lock causes incorrect final distances
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the fundamental limitation of Dijkstra's algorithm

diagrams.title: Shortest Path Tree Construction
diagrams.description: Shows how the predecessor array builds a tree of shortest paths
elements: Each vertex points to its parent on shortest path
Tree has exactly V-1 edges for V reachable vertices
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how to reconstruct and visualize the actual shortest paths
